./comments_pr890.md:7:PR #890 adds a `TraceID` field (`json:"trace_id,omitempty"`) to the `WatsonRequest` struct alongside the existing `ControlTransactionID`. The field captures UUIDv7 trace IDs minted by Control (Kochava/control#489) to enable trace-based log correlation in reconciliation logs and traffic replay tooling. The change is backward compatible (absent field → empty string) and passes all existing tests.
./comments_pr890.md:17:**Backward Compatibility**: The `omitempty` tag ensures callers that don't send `trace_id` won't trigger unnecessary JSON serialization, preserving exact-match validation in handler tests and reclog consumers.
./comments_pr890.md:29:2. **`omitempty` behavior**: Critical for backward compatibility. Callers that don't include `trace_id` in their payloads will see the field as an empty string when deserialized, but it won't be re-serialized into responses or logs unless explicitly set. This is the correct approach for optional fields that are part of a larger data object.
./comments_pr890.md:49:**Merge.** This is a well-executed struct extension that enablesTrace ID tracing across the attribution pipeline without breaking existing clients or test fixtures. The follow-up commit (9fe96f58, "fix: omit empty trace_id from serialized requests") correctly ensures that empty trace IDs don't pollute test fixtures or reclog data.
